query: US spot Bitcoin ETF net flows late June 2026 and early July 2026, weekly trend from Farside Investors and SoSoValue. Are net outflows continuing into the week of June 29-July 3 2026? What is the Bitcoin price trend in late June 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**ETF Flows — Week of June 29–July 3, 2026:** Yes, net outflows continued. US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded **~$526–527 million in net outflows** for the week (four trading sessions; July 3 was a holiday), extending the streak to an **eighth consecutive week**. Three heavy-selling sessions (June 29–July 1) were partially offset by a **late reversal on July 2 (+$221.7M)** — the largest single-day inflow in two months, which snapped a 10-session, ~$2.73B outflow streak. Notably, FBTC led the reversal (~$166M) while IBIT still bled (~$40M). June 2026 was the **worst month on record** (~$4.06–4.5B net outflows). The eight-week streak finally ended the following week (July 7–11, ~$282M combined inflows).

**Bitcoin Price — Late June 2026:** A sharp downtrend. BTC fell **~20.5% in June** (steepest monthly drop since June 2022), touching **21-month lows near $58,000** on June 25 and June 30. Early July brought stabilization/rebound: from $58,558 (June 30 close) up to ~$62,544 (July 3) and ~$64,127 (July 10), trading ~$63,832 on July 12. The rebound coincided with the ETF inflow reversal and a soft June jobs report easing Fed rate concerns.

Full synthesis, data points, and sources have been captured in the structured output.

sources.title: U.S. Spot Bitcoin ETFs Record $527M Net Outflows From June 29 to July 2
sources.snippet: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs shed roughly $527 million in net outflows across the four trading sessions from June 29 to July 2, 2026, extending a weeks-long withdrawal streak before a late reversal.

sources.title: Bitcoin ETFs See Over $526 Million in Outflows Over Four Days as Eight-Week Streak Continues
sources.snippet: Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $526.64 million in outflows for the week from June 29 to July 3. Outflows continued for eight consecutive weeks.

sources.title: Bitcoin ETF outflows stretch to eighth week as altcoin funds draw cash
sources.snippet: Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $527 million in net outflows from June 29 to July 2, extending their weekly withdrawal streak to eight weeks.

sources.title: Finally: $221 Million Flows Into Bitcoin ETFs, Ending a Painful 10-Day Outflow Streak
sources.snippet: U.S.-listed bitcoin ETFs saw $221.7 million in inflows on Thursday, their largest daily intake in two months. FBTC led with ~$166M while IBIT recorded a $40.43M outflow. YTD net outflows remain ~$5.4B.

sources.title: Bitcoin ETF News: Inflows Hit $221M as 10-Day Outflow Streak Ends
sources.snippet: US-listed spot BTC ETFs pulled in $221M on July 3 2026, snapping a 10-day outflow streak that drained $2.73Bn, per SoSoValue. BTC rebounded to ~$61,700 after 21-month lows under $58,000.

sources.title: BlackRock's IBIT Led $4.06B June Exodus After BTC ETF News
sources.snippet: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $4.06 billion in net outflows during June 2026 per SoSoValue, largest monthly redemption since launch; IBIT ~$3.3B (~75%). Final week of June ~$1.79B redemptions.

sources.title: Bitcoin Spot ETFs Post Worst Month on Record With $4.5 Billion June Outflow
sources.snippet: US-listed Bitcoin ETFs recorded $4.5 billion in net outflows during June 2026, worst monthly figure since Jan 2024. Bitcoin fell 20.48% over the month, steepest since June 2022.

sources.title: Bitcoin and ether ETFs pull in $282M, snapping eight-week outflow streak
sources.snippet: US spot Bitcoin and ether ETFs recorded ~$282 million combined net inflow during the week of July 7-11, ending the eight-week outflow streak.

sources.title: Bitcoin ETF Flow (US$m) – Farside Investors
sources.snippet: Daily flow table: 23 Jun 2026 -113.8; 24 Jun 2026 -469.0 (IBIT -239.3, FBTC -120.8).

sources.title: Bitcoin ETF Inflows Hit $510M Over 3 Days
sources.snippet: US spot Bitcoin ETFs pulled in ~$510 million across three inflow sessions July 2–7, ending a 10-day, $2.73 billion outflow streak.
